title: Provider-lock cleanup — cloudy README + SUPERPOWERS proposals
description: Child of vykean-hq/T-VHQ-092. Smallest per-repo scope: README.md (7 hits) + SUPERPOWERS_IMPROVEMENTS_PROPOSAL.md (2 hits) + SUPERPOWERS_PLANNING_PHASE_PROPOSAL.md (1 hit). Install pre-commit enforcement.

context.text: Cross-repo parent: vykean-hq/T-VHQ-092. Sweep report: vykean-hq/PROVIDER_LOCK_SWEEP.md. Cloudy is the execution layer — it doesn't own engine selection (omnai does), so any reference to a specific engine in cloudy docs is either (a) describing the default sweech binding — move to a 'configurable via sweechProfile' note, or (b) a legacy hardcoded expectation that needs rewriting. SCOPE: README.md + 2 SUPERPOWERS proposals only — do NOT edit src/ (the audit sweep did not flag src hits for this task).

Shipped (commit 11fff7d). README.md: 8 Claude-Code mentions neutralised — `Claude Code (default)` section kept with inline `provider-lock:allow arch-comparison` hatch (legit arch point: it's the default binding for the `claude-code` engine); all other mentions rewritten to engine-agnostic (`<supervisorSweechProfile>`, `the executing engine`, `agent-CLI session`). SUPERPOWERS_IMPROVEMENTS_PROPOSAL.md: 10 `Claude` refs rewritten engine-agnostic (executing engine, reviewing engine, planning engine). SUPERPOWERS_PLANNING_PHASE_PROPOSAL.md: 7 prose `Claude` refs rewritten; `ClaudeModel` TS type identifier left (it's a code symbol, not lock-in prose). Pre-commit hook installed + verified via commit. scripts/check-provider-lock.sh: fixed `.test.(ts|tsx|js|swift|py)$` anchor bug (the `(^|/)` prefix broke it); added `package.json` to EXEMPT_PATH_PATTERN (per script's own header — 'SDK package manifests' exempt); added content-level exempt for `@anthropic-ai/*-sdk` npm package identifiers (same semantic as manifests). These fixes should be upstreamed to vykean-hq/scripts/check-provider-lock.sh in a follow-up. CARVE-OUT: 3 src/ violations remain out of scope per task guard rails (NEVER touch src/) — src/reviewer.ts:248 docstring `using Claude`, src/dashboard/client/DaemonApp.tsx:5206 user-facing error message `use Claude Code directly`, DaemonApp.tsx:5969 GPT-4 trivia Easter-egg string. These are genuine src lock-in that need a follow-up task to add inline `provider-lock:allow` hatches or rewrite. Default full-repo scan still shows these 3; staged/pre-commit scan passes clean.

acceptanceCriteria: scripts/check-provider-lock.sh + scripts/install-hooks.sh copied from vykean-hq; pre-commit hook installed
`bash scripts/check-provider-lock.sh .` on this repo exits 0 on default pattern
README.md rewrite: 7 flagged hits neutralised — any 'Claude does X' becomes '<engine per sweechProfile> does X' OR inline-hatched arch-comparison when it's a genuine architectural point
SUPERPOWERS_IMPROVEMENTS_PROPOSAL.md + SUPERPOWERS_PLANNING_PHASE_PROPOSAL.md: proposal language made engine-agnostic

If cloudy has its own `cloudy.yaml` or similar runtime config that hardcodes a profile name, leave it with an inline `provider-lock:allow user-config-pending-T-VHQ-095` hatch (same pattern as vykean-hq/loopy.yaml)
quality_gates: Pre-commit hook live; negative smoke passes
